findings.title: migrateRule accepts any -- local copy is typed looser than the canonical version in StrategyBuilder
findings.detail: StrategyComparison defines migrateRule(rule: any) with no return type annotation. The canonical version in StrategyBuilder.tsx is migrateRule(rule: Rule): Rule. The StrategyComparison copy also sets migrated.params (plural) at line 24 while StrategyBuilder uses migrated.param (singular) -- this mutation silently writes an undeclared property and never sets the correctly-named one, which the checker misses entirely because rule is any. MA indicator rules migrated through this path will be silently broken at runtime.
findings.fix: Replace the local migrateRule with an import of the shared utility, or at minimum match the StrategyBuilder signature (rule: Rule): Rule and fix the params->param typo.

findings.title: Equity curve map uses two unsafe casts after a filter that does not narrow the type
findings.detail: TimeValue.value is typed as number | null. The .filter(d => d.value !== null) call does not narrow d.value to number in TypeScript without a type predicate, so the subsequent .map uses time: d.time as any and value: d.value as number to force the cast. The as any on time is the more dangerous of the two -- lightweight-charts expects a specific Time type and as any silently accepts any shape.
findings.fix: Replace .filter(d => d.value !== null) with .filter((d): d is { time: string; value: number } => d.value !== null) and remove both as-casts from the map.

findings.title: ComparisonResult is defined locally and also exported from shared/types -- duplicate type with no link
findings.detail: index.ts exports ComparisonResult at line 244. StrategyComparison.tsx declares its own local interface ComparisonResult at line 47 with identical shape but no import from shared/types. The two are structurally equivalent today but will drift independently.
findings.fix: Remove the local ComparisonResult interface and import it from shared/types.

findings.title: loadSavedStrategies is duplicated from StrategyBuilder with weaker typing and diverged migration logic
findings.detail: StrategyBuilder.tsx already has an identically-named loadSavedStrategies(): SavedStrategy[] reading the same SAVED_STRATEGIES_KEY. The StrategyComparison copy uses JSON.parse(raw) with an implicit assertion to SavedStrategy[] (no runtime validation) and calls the any-typed local migrateRule. Migration logic now has two maintenance surfaces.
findings.fix: Extract loadSavedStrategies and migrateRule into a shared utility module (e.g. frontend/src/features/strategy/savedStrategies.ts) and import from both files.

findings.title: METRICS format callbacks typed as (v: any) -- silently permits "undefined%" rendering for optional summary fields
findings.detail: For the four metrics using v?.toFixed(N) inside a template literal, if v is undefined the output is the string "undefined%" because the optional chain evaluates to undefined and template coercion stringifies it. The val != null guard at the call site protects required fields today, but adding an optional metric in the future would silently regress.
findings.fix: Type the format callback as (v: number | null | undefined) => string and replace v?.toFixed(N) inside template literals with v != null ? v.toFixed(N) : "--".

findings.title: SessionBucket is a local type duplicate of the anonymous shape in BacktestResult[session_analytics]
findings.detail: index.ts defines BacktestResult.session_analytics with an anonymous inline array element type. Results.tsx defines an identical local SessionBucket type. These are two independent sources of truth -- a field rename in index.ts requires a matching edit in Results.tsx.
findings.fix: Export SessionAnalyticsBucket from index.ts, use it in BacktestResult.session_analytics, and remove the local SessionBucket type in Results.tsx.

findings.title: fmtBucketRange assumes HH:MM format with no input validation -- produces NaN output on malformed bucket strings
findings.detail: fmtBucketRange splits on ":" and maps to Number. If the backend returns a bucket string in any other format, h and m are NaN, totalMins is NaN, and the function returns a string like "09:30-NaN:NaN" without any error.
findings.fix: Add a guard after splitting: if parts.length !== 2 or either Number() is NaN, return b unchanged as a safe fallback.

findings.title: runComparison has no stale-response guard -- results from a superseded run can land on active state
findings.detail: runComparison is a plain async function with no AbortController, no mounted-flag, and no request ID. If the user triggers a comparison, toggles compareMode off and on while requests are in-flight, and results resolve, setResults fires on the new instance and can show stale results from a previous ticker or interval.
findings.fix: Introduce a request-ID counter incremented on each call; capture the local ID before await and discard the response if the counter has moved on before setResults.

findings.title: Chart height captured once from clientHeight at effect execution -- dynamic sizing does not work
findings.detail: The chart is created with height: chartRef.current.clientHeight || 240. In practice the div has a fixed inline height of 240px so clientHeight is always 240, making the branch harmless but misleading. No ResizeObserver is wired.
findings.fix: Pass the literal 240 directly to createChart to make the intent explicit, or wire a ResizeObserver to call chart.applyOptions({ height }) on size changes.

residual_risks: The migration logic in StrategyComparison.tsx sets migrated.params (plural) at line 24 while StrategyBuilder uses migrated.param (singular). This writes an undeclared property and the correctly-named property is never set when a MA rule is migrated. Runtime tests against the comparison backtest path with MA indicator strategies are needed to confirm migrated rules are not silently broken.

Promise.all in runComparison fires concurrent POSTs to /api/backtest. Three simultaneous backtests on uncached date ranges may contend on yfinance. Not a correctness issue but worth verifying under concurrent load.

The ComparisonResult exported from shared/types is never imported anywhere -- it is a dead export. If a future component imports it while StrategyComparison retains its local definition, structural compatibility is guaranteed today but is a latent maintenance hazard.
testing_gaps: No test covers fmtBucketRange in Results.tsx -- the NaN path (malformed bucket string) is untested and will silently render "HH:MM-NaN:NaN" in the best/worst summary.
winRateColor does not clamp inputs -- a win_rate value outside [0, 100] will produce RGB channel values outside 0-255. The function has no test for boundary or out-of-range values.
runComparison has no test for partial Promise.all failure -- if one of three backtest requests rejects, all three results are discarded and only the first rejection message is shown.

SessionAnalytics renders null when data is empty -- there is no test confirming the null return path does not leave a dangling borderTop divider in the Results layout when session_analytics is an empty array.
